spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (window., SharedWorker) with runtime script (~5700 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (3 buttons / 1 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (both, cannot, document, list, policy, requests, worker).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: Page does not include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link — violates the rule #3 self-heal invariant.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 93 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link
openQuestions.detail: Page is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/5175745573945344 link inside a `references` section. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires this on every concept so the slug-recovery script can re-derive the canonical name.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Connection Allowlists is a feature designed to provide explicit control over external endpoints by restricting connections initiated via the Fetch API or other web platform APIs from a document or worker.  The proposed implementation involv") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://wicg.github.io/connection-allowlists to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~5700 chars of script across 3 button(s) and 1 other control(s). hits 3 pass / 1 partial / 2 fail across the six rubric criteria.
